content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF

NIELSEN, MERKSAMER,
HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

SACRAMENTO

591 REDWOOD HIGHWAY, #4000

SAN FRANCISCO

770 L STREET, SUITE 800
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752

MILL VALLEY, CALIFORNIA 94941
TELEPHONE (415) 389-6800

650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
TELEPHONE (415) 989-6800

May 8, 1991


Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
Chief, Technical Assistance
& Analysis Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807


Re:  Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice

Dear Jeanne:

     This letter requests written confirmation of our recent
telephone conversation regarding the sender identification
requirements of a slate mailer organization.  In our
conversation, I indicated that a client of this firm has been
asked to make a payment to a slate mailer organization to support
several candidates and a measure on a slate mailer not produced
by the slate mailer organization.  The slate mailer organization
has been in existence for a number of years and done mailings in
a number of prior elections.  I indicated that the payment by our
client would not be "at the behest" of any of the candidates nor
of the ballot measure committee and therefore would, in our
opinion, constitute an independent expenditure to support those
candidates and the measure.

     I also indicated that the payment being requested from our
client may be more than 50% of all payments received for the
slate mailer.  Our client is advised by the slate mailer
organization that other candidates and measures will be making
payments to the slate mailer organization to appear on the slate
mailer and the slate mailer organization may also put some
candidates and measures on the slate without having received a
payment.

     I advised you that we understand that the slate mailer
organization complies with Government Code Section 82408.4; that
it will produce one or more slate mailers, as that term is
defined in Government Code Section 82048.3; that is has
registered with the California Secretary of State pursuant to
Government Code Section 84108; that it intends to disclose its
activities pursuant to Government Code Section 84218 and 84219;
and that if it receives contributions immediately prior to the
election it will file reports pursuant to Government Code Section
84220.

Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
May 8, 1991
Page 2


     Based on the above facts we asked the following questions
and you provided the following answers:

     1.     Question: What "sender identification" is required on
                     the slate mailer produced by the slate
                     organization?

            Answer:    The slate mailer organization must meet the
                       identification and disclaimer requirements of
                       Government Code Section 84305.5.
                       Specifically, the slate mailer must include
                       the name, street address and city of the
                       slate mailer organization on the outside of
                       each piece of slate mail and on at least one
                       of the inserts in no less than 8 point roman
                       type which shall be in a color or print which
                       contrasts with the background so as to be
                       easily legible. At the top or bottom of the
                       front side or surface of at least one insert
                       or at the top or bottom of one side or
                       surface of a post card or other self mailer
                       there is a notice of at least 8 point roman
                       bold face type notice which shall be in a
                       color or print which contrasts with the
                       background so as to be easily legible and
                       drawn in a box. The notice shall consist of
                       the following statement:

                                    Notice to Voters

                       This document was prepared by (Name of State Mailer Organization), not an
                       official political party organization. Appearance in this mailer does not
                       necessarily imply endorsement of others in this mailer. Appearances paid
                       for and authorized by each candidate and ballot measure which is designated
                       by *.


     2.     Question: If our client's payment constitutes the
                     majority of all payments received for the
                     production of the slate mailer, is the mass
                     mailing identification number described in
                     Government Code Section 84305 required?

            Answer:    No. The requirement for the slate mailer
                       organization takes precedent.

Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
May 8, 1991
Page 3




3.    Question: Can the "Notice to Voters" be modified to add
           a sentence to identify payments received by
           the slate mailer organization which
           constitute "independent expenditures", as
           that term is defined in Government Code
           Section 82031, to support candidates or
           support or oppose ballot measures?

      Answer:   The slate mailer statutes do not contemplate
                a payment to a slate mailer organization
                which is an independent expenditure and
                instead only contemplate direct payments by
                candidate or ballot measure committees or
                payments by third parties at the behest of
                candidates or ballot measure committees. You
                indicated that this additional language to
                the Notice to Voters, while not required by
                the statute, does provide further and more
                complete disclosure of the transactions.
                Such additional disclosure would be
                considered in furtherance of the Act.

I believe that this letter accurately reflects our
telephone conversation. Please provide written confirmation of
this telephone advice at your earliest convenience. If you have
any questions or need any additional information, please do not
hesitate to contact me.

                                        Kind regards,

                                        Kirk Alan Pessner
                                        Paralegal/Administrator
                                        Political Reports Unit

KAP/cn
